changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforced to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's requirement to ensure secure transmission of access authorization information. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor adjustment was made for clarity. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security features that enforce TLS for secure communication.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'required' instead of 'enforced' for consistency with other naming conventions
title: Access Control Decisions - Access control decisions | transmit access authorization information transmit [
step1_reasoning: The control AC-24(1) focuses on ensuring that access authorization information is securely transmitted. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure transmission through TLS for API server communications, enforcing TLS in NetworkPolicies for secure pod-to-pod communication, enabling TLS for Ingress resources to secure external traffic, and using mutual TLS (mTLS) in service meshes for secure service-to-service communication.
